Denominator Definition: Children 0-23 months of age
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Continued Breastfeeding (12-15 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of children 12–15 months of age who were fed with breast milk during the previous day.
Numerator Definition: Children 12-15 months of age who were fed breast milk during the previous day
Denominator Definition: Children 12-15 months of age
Additional Indicator Details: This indicator has been replaced with the new indicator - Continued breastfeeding among children 12-23 months of age. It is  recommended that this new indicator be disaggregated and reported for the age-groups of 12-15months, 16-19months and 20-23months.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Breastfeeding Area Graphs: Weaned (0 - 23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of infants 0-23 months of age who did not receive any breastmilk
Numerator Definition: Number of infants 0-23 months of age who were not breastfed
Denominator Definition: Infants 0-23 months of age
Additional Indicator Details: Oral rehydration salts, vitamins, minerals or medicines are not considered part of any feeding category.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Continued Breastfeeding (20-23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of children 20-23 months of age who were fed breast milk during the previous day
Numerator Definition: Children 20-23 months of age who were fed breast milk during the previous day
Denominator Definition: Children 20-23 months of age
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Breastfeeding Area Graphs: Exclusive Breastfeeding (0-23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of infants 0-23 months of age who received only breastmilk
Numerator Definition: Number of infants 0-23 months of age who were fed only breastmilk
Denominator Definition: Infants 0-23 months of age
Additional Indicator Details:
Children that were only fed breast milk in the 24 hours before the survey. These infants would not have received water-based liquids, milk based-liquids or food.
Oral rehydration salts, vitamins, minerals or medicines are not considered part of any feeding category.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Breastfeeding Area Graphs: Breastfeeding and Plain Water (0-23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of infants 0-23 months of age who received breastmilk and plain water
Numerator Definition: Number of infants 0-23 months of age who were fed breastmilk and plain water
Denominator Definition: Infants 0-23 months of age
Additional Indicator Details: Children that were fed breast milk plus plain water in the 24 hours before the survey.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Breastfeeding Area Graphs: Breastfeeding and Other Liquids (0-23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of infants 0-23 months of age who received breastmilk and non-milk liquids
Numerator Definition: Number of infants 0-23 months of age who were fed breastmilk and non-milk liquids
Denominator Definition: Infants 0-23 months of age
Additional Indicator Details:
Children fed breast milk plus non milk liquids (e.g. juice, herbal tea, sweetened water, flavored water, etc.) in the 24 hours before the survey. Children in this category may also have been fed plain water.
Oral rehydration salts, vitamins, minerals or medicines are not considered part of any feeding category.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Breastfeeding Area Graphs: Breastfeeding and Dairy (0-23 months)
Alternate (Display) Name: Breastmilk and other milk / formula (0-23 months)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of infants 0-23 months of age who received breastmilk and formula/other milk
Numerator Definition: Number of infants 0-23 months of age who were fed breastmilk and formula/other milk
Denominator Definition: Infants 0-23 months of age
Additional Indicator Details:
Children fed breast milk plus formula and/or other milk (e.g. infant formula, liquid milk, canned milk, powdered milk, etc.) in the 24 hours before the survey. Children in this category may also have been fed plain water and/or non-milk liquids.
Oral rehydration salts, vitamins, minerals or medicines are not considered part of any feeding category.

Indicator Name: Prevalence of low birth weight among new-borns (Modeled Estimates)
Alternate (Display) Name: Percentage of infants weighing less than 2,500 grams at birth
Indicator Definition: Percentage of live births under 2500 g out of the total number of live births during the same period.
Numerator Definition: Number of live births with weight less than 2500 g
Denominator Definition: Total number of live births
Additional Indicator Details:
WHO defines low birth weight as less than 2500 g (5.5 lb)​

Survey data on low birth weight are limited since many infants in some low-income countries are born at home and not weighed at birth and even among those weighed some do not have a record of their borthweight or the caregiver is uable to recall the birthweight at time of survey intevriew. However, methods agreed upon by an inter-agency group have been applied to survey data to adjust for missing birthweights and data heaping. 

b-spline: data for countries with ≥8 data points from high coverage administrative sources with ≥1 prior to 2005 and ≥1 more recent than 2010, were smoothed with b-spline regression to generate annual LBW prevalence estimates that followed country-reported estimates very closely.

Hierarchical regression: data for countries not meeting requirements for b-spline but with ≥1 LBW country input data point were fitted into a model using a set of covariates to generate annual LBW prevalence estimates. The covariates included the natural log of neonatal mortality rate; the proportion of children underweight (weight for-age z score below minus two standard deviations from median weight for age of reference population); data type (high coverage administrative, low coverage administrative, household survey); UN region (e.g., Southern Asia, Caribbean); and a country-specific random effect.).  These estimates may vary substantially from those reported by countries.

Partial data: the estimate is based on only partial data for the most recent survey, therefore modelled estimates not shown for the individual country.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF, World Health Organization

Indicator Name: Height-for-age <-2SD but weight-for-height ≥-2SD and weight-for-height ≤2SD

Denominator Definition: Children under 5 years of age in the surveyed population
Additional Indicator Details: This is a derived indicator calculated by subtracting sum of the point estimate of stunted and overweight and the point estimate of stunted and wasted from the point of estimate of stunting (all children with valid HAZ)
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Children without a birth weight
Indicator Definition: Percentage of births without a recorded or reported birth weight
Numerator Definition: Number of births without a recorded or reported birth weight
Denominator Definition: Number of births in the recall period
Additional Indicator Details:
Calculation: Numerator divided by denominator multiplied by 100
Handling of missing values: Included in denominator
Notes:
For DHS, the definition of included births changed in 2018, moving from all live births in the last five years to the most recent live-birth in the last five years. The MICS definition, most recent live birth in the last two years, has not changed.

The recall period can vary by survey. DHS recall period is typically five years, while for MICS it is usually two years.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF, World Health Organization

Numerator Definition: Number of non-breastfed children 6-23 months of age who received foods from ≥ 5 (out of 8) food groups during the previous day
Denominator Definition: Non-breastfed children aged 6-23 months
Additional Indicator Details: The eight food groups are: (i) breastmilk; (ii) grains, roots and tubers; (iii) legumes and nuts; (iv) dairy products (infant formula, milk, yogurt, cheese); (v) flesh foods (meat, fish, poultry and liver/organ meats); (vi) eggs; (vii) vitamin-A rich fruits and vegetables; (viii) other fruits and vegetables.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Indicator Name: Height-for-age <-2 SD (Stunting) Modeled Estimates

Denominator Definition: Children under 5 years of age in the surveyed population with valid height-for-age z-scores
Additional Indicator Details: This indicator is generated from country-level modelled estimates
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF, World Bank, World Health Organization

Indicator Name: Prevalence of anaemia in children aged 6–59 months (Modeled Estimates)
Indicator Definition: Percentage of children aged 6−59 months with a haemoglobin concentration less than 110 g/L, adjusted for altitude.
Numerator Definition: Number of children aged 6−59 months with a haemoglobin concentration less than 110 g/L, adjusted for altitude.
Denominator Definition: Number of children aged 6-59 that were assessed
Additional Indicator Details:
The anaemia status of children is assessed using blood haemoglobin concentrations. In surveys, blood haemoglobin concentrations are typically measured using the direct cyanmethemoglobin method in a laboratory or with a portable, battery-operated, haemoglobin photometer in the field that uses the azide-methaemoglobin method.


Prevalence of anaemia and/or mean haemoglobin in children under 5 years were obtained from 283 population-representative data sources from 111 countries worldwide. Data collected from 1990 to 2016 were used. A Bayesian hierarchical mixture model was used to estimate haemoglobin distributions and systematically addressed missing data, non-linear time trends, and representativeness of data sources. Full details on data sources will be available in WHO’s forthcoming report on anaemia prevalence: Trends in anaemia in women and children: 1995 to 2016 (WHO, 2017). Full details on statistical methods may be found here: Global, regional, and national trends in haemoglobin concentration and prevalence of total and severe anaemia in children and pregnant and non-pregnant women for 1995–2011: a systematic analysis of population-representative data (Stevens et al, 2013).
Custodian(s): World Health Organization

Alternate (Display) Name: Prevalence of anaemia in pregnant women
Indicator Definition: Percentage of women aged 15−49 years with a haemoglobin concentration less than 120 g/L for non-pregnant women and lactating women, and less than 110 g/L for pregnant women, adjusted for altitude and smoking.
Numerator Definition: Number of women aged 15−49 years with a haemoglobin concentration less than 120 g/L for non-pregnant women and lactating women, and less than 110 g/L for pregnant women, adjusted for altitude and smoking.
Denominator Definition: Total number of pregnant women aged 15-49 years with haemoglobin assessed
Additional Indicator Details:
The anaemia status of women is assessed using blood haemoglobin concentrations. In surveys, blood haemoglobin concentrations are typically measured using the direct cyanmethemoglobin method in a laboratory or with a portable, battery-operated, haemoglobin photometer in the field that uses the azide-methaemoglobin method.

Prevalence of anaemia and/or mean haemoglobin in women of reproductive age were obtained from 303 population-representative data sources from 116 countries worldwide. Data collected from 1990 to 2016 were used. A Bayesian hierarchical mixture model was used to estimate haemoglobin distributions and systematically addressed missing data, non-linear time trends, and representativeness of data sources. Custodian(s): World Health Organization

Numerator Definition: Percent distribution of women with a birth in last 5 years who took iron tablets or syrup for 90 or more days during pregnancy of last birth.
Denominator Definition: Number of women with a birth in the 5 years preceding the survey

Mixed milk feeding includes any formula (e.g. infant formula, follow-on formula, “toddler milk”) or any liquid animal milk other than human breast milk, (e.g. cow’s milk, goat’s milk, evaporated milk or reconstituted powdered milk).

Breastfeeding by a wet nurse, feeding of expressed breast milk and feeding of donor human milk all count as being fed breast milk.

Yogurt, whether liquid or solid, is not counted here because it is not generally given as a substitute for breast milk.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF

Children with a birth weight
Indicator Definition: Percentage of births with a recorded or reported birth weight
Numerator Definition: ​Number of births with a recorded or reported birth weight
Denominator Definition: ​Number of births in the recall period
Additional Indicator Details:
Calculation: Numerator divided by denominator multiplied by 100
Handling of missing values: Included in denominator
Notes:
For DHS, the definition of included births changed in 2018, moving from all live births in the last five years to the most recent live-birth in the last five years. The MICS definition, most recent live birth in the last two years, has not changed.

This indicator differs slightly from MICS Indicator TM.11, which includes children whose caretaker reported that the child was weighed at birth, but did not provide a specific weight. This indicator includes only children whose birth weight was specified.
The recall period can vary by survey. DHS recall period is typically five years, while for MICS it is usually two years.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF, World Health Organization

Indicator Name: Children with a valid birth weight
Indicator Definition: Percentage of births with a valid recorded or reported birth weight
Numerator Definition:
​Survey: Number of births with a reported birthweight between 250 to 5,500 grams

Administrative: Annual number of live births with an ‘in-range’ birthweight
Denominator Definition:
​Survey: Number of births in the recall period

Administrative: Estimated annual number of live births
Additional Indicator Details:
Calculation: Numerator divided by denominator multiplied by 100
Handling of missing values: Included in denominator
Notes:
For DHS, the definition of included births changed in 2018, moving from all live births in the last five years to the most recent live-birth in the last five years. The MICS definition, most recent live birth in the last two years, has not changed.

The recall period can vary by survey. DHS recall period is typically five years, while for MICS it is usually two years.

For surveys, valid means that the birthweight is biologically plausible, and is defined as between 250g and 5,500g. For administrative data, valid means that the birthweight fits within the 'valid range' as specified by the country; and the 'valid range' varies by country.
The denominator for administrative data is an estimation/projection from the UN Population Division World Population Prospects
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF, World Health Organization

Indicator Name: Moderate Food Poverty
Indicator Definition: Percentage of children 6–23 months of age consuming foods and beverages from three or four out of eight defined food groups during the previous day
Numerator Definition: ​Children 6-23 months of age who consumed 3 or 4 food groups (out of 8) during the previous day
Denominator Definition: ​Children 6-23 months of age.
Additional Indicator Details: The eight food groups are: (i) breastmilk; (ii) grains, roots, tubers and plantains; (iii) pulses (beans, peas, lentils), nuts and seeds; (iv) dairy products (infant formula, milk, yogurt, cheese); (v) flesh foods (meat, fish, poultry, organ meats); (vi) eggs; (vii) vitamin-A rich fruits and vegetables; (viii) other fruits and vegetables.
Custodian(s): Division of Analysis, Planning and Monitoring, UNICEF
